mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery TD5 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned TD5 engine typically costs £3,600–£4,500 supply only. Fitting adds £900–£1,200 depending on the specialist. A full fitted replacement from a specialist workshop costs approximately £5,980 including VAT. Used engines can be found from £800–£1,400, but condition varies dramatically.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery TD5 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the TD5 is widely regarded as one of the most dependable Land Rover engines ever built. It has a strong cast-iron block, simple five-cylinder design, and no timing belt to worry about. Many have exceeded 200,000 miles with proper maintenance. The oil pump bolt on early 10P engines is its one critical vulnerability — address that and the TD5 is exceptionally durable.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery TD5?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main issues are the oil pump bolt working loose on early 10P engines (catastrophic if ignored), injector wiring harness oil contamination, cracked cylinder heads on early TD5s, and diesel dilution from leaking injector seals. All are well understood and repairable — the TD5 has no fundamental design flaws beyond the oil pump bolt.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery TD5 engine, or should I scrap the vehicle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the vehicle's condition. Against a well-maintained Discovery 2 worth £3,000–£8,000, a £4,500–£5,500 replacement may be 55–70% of the car's value — borderline but often worth it for a cherished example. With only 76 TD5 Discoveries still on UK roads, well-maintained examples are becoming collectable. For heavily corroded examples, get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery TD5 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: TD5 engines are identified by the prefix on the engine number: 10P (early, 1998–2002) or 15P (late, 2002–2004). The engine number is stamped on the block. Early 10P engines are the ones with the oil pump bolt vulnerability — confirm this has been addressed.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the TD5 oil pump bolt, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The bolt must be replaced with a correctly torqued and thread-locked replacement — it's a known manufacturing issue on early 10P engines. A preventative replacement costs £200–£400. A failed bolt almost always destroys the bearings and crankshaft, requiring a £3,600–£5,500 engine replacement.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery TD5 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Bearing and crank damage typically means replacement
Worth it on a well-maintained Discovery
Injector seal replacement; catch it early

buyingChecks.title: Buying This TD5? Check These First
buyIf: * Oil pump bolt replaced (critical — confirm on any early 10P engine)
* Injector wiring harness checked and free of oil contamination
* No signs of overheating or coolant loss (head gasket/cylinder head health)
* Full service history with regular oil changes (TD5s are sensitive to oil quality)
avoidIf: * No record of oil pump bolt work on a 10P engine — assume it's overdue
* Oil in the red ECU plug — injector harness contamination is likely
* Overheating history or pressurised coolant system — head may be cracked
* Excessive black smoke or hard starting — injection pump or injector issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The TD5 is one of Land Rover's most respected engines — a 2.5-litre five-cylinder turbo-diesel with a cast-iron block and no timing belt to worry about. Its reputation is built on torque and durability, not refinement. The oil pump bolt on early engines is its one critical vulnerability — address that and the TD5 will outlast the chassis around it. With only 76 TD5 Discoveries still on UK roads, well-maintained examples are becoming increasingly collectable.

cta.label: → Compare TD5 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery TD5 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Oil Pump Bolt — Confirm replacement on any 10P engine (critical — ask for proof)
* Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; any hesitation or rough idle points to injector or harness issues
* ECU Connector — Check the red plug for oil contamination (injector harness warning)
* Coolant System — Check for pressurisation, oil in coolant, or coolant loss (head gasket/head crack warning)
* Smoke Check — Black smoke = over-fuelling; white smoke = coolant ingress; blue smoke = oil burning
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lack of power or excessive smoke?
* Service History — Including oil change intervals (TD5s are sensitive to oil quality) — see the map above
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures and structural corrosion (Discovery 2 chassis are known to rust)
